topic.prompt_en: Describe an activity or habit that shapes your daily life.
topic.learning_goal: Learn to describe a repeated habit through origin, routine, one benefit, and a believable example.

angles.title: Name the habit simply
content: Choose one habit you actually do often enough to explain naturally.
Say when you started or why it became part of your life.

angles.title: Describe the routine
content: Mention when, how long, and in what setting you do it.
Small routine details make the answer sound real.

angles.title: Show one benefit
content: Focus on one main effect such as better mood, clearer thinking, or more energy.
Avoid claiming that the habit changed your whole life.

angles.title: Use one example
content: Give one day or period when the habit clearly helped.
This proves the value without sounding exaggerated.

sequence.target: Name the habit and how it started.
Explain how you do it and what it adds to your day.
End with a realistic reason the habit still matters.

high_score_tips: Simple daily habits often sound better than unusual hobbies because the details are easier to support.
Use one measurable routine detail such as after dinner, for twenty minutes, or three times a week.
Keep the benefit modest and believable.
If the habit is ordinary, the reflection needs to be clear.

content.problem: Confusing a habit with a life philosophy
content.why_it_hurts: The answer becomes abstract too early and loses concrete detail.
content.fix: Start with what you do, when you do it, and why it fits your routine.

content.problem: Talking about many habits at once
content.why_it_hurts: The answer loses focus and never develops one idea properly.
content.fix: Choose one habit and build depth through example and effect.

content.why_it_hurts: The answer can sound forced if a small habit supposedly changed everything.
content.fix: Describe one realistic benefit such as better sleep, calmer evenings, or clearer thinking.

language.wrong_example: I insist myself to walk every night.
language.better_example: I make myself go for a walk every night.
language.reason: Make myself is a natural spoken pattern. Insist myself is not.

language.wrong_example: My hobby is walking every night after dinner because it is a habit.
language.better_example: It is more of a habit than a hobby because I do it to reset after work.
language.reason: Differentiate hobby and habit when needed instead of treating them as identical.

core.example: The habit fits naturally into my evening routine.
I have done it consistently for nearly a year.
It helps me reset after a long day.
It improves my energy level in the evening.
The habit helps me maintain a better rhythm.
It is a small form of discipline that actually feels manageable.

extended.note: Useful when the habit helps after work.
Good alternative to easy or simple.
Useful when the habit is helpful because it is easy to keep.
A very common spoken phrase for habits and plans.
Band 8 style phrase for habits that create stability.

core.example: I usually go for a walk after dinner.
The habit helps me wind down before the evening ends.
Even twenty minutes of walking helps clear my mind.
I have managed to stick to it because it is simple.
It sets the tone for the rest of my evening.
It is a small habit, but it makes a real difference.

extended.example: It is nothing fancy, but it is surprisingly effective.

extended.note: Good when explaining why a simple routine works.
extended.example: I keep it short so the habit stays sustainable.

extended.meaning_zh: 把一天切开，打断疲惫感
extended.note: Natural phrase for walks, stretches, or reading breaks.
extended.example: It helps break up the day if work feels too heavy.

extended.meaning_zh: 给我一个头脑重置的机会
extended.note: Strong but still natural reflective phrase.
extended.example: That short walk gives me a mental reset.

framework.goal: Describe one repeatable habit through routine, benefit, and realistic value.

moves: Name the habit and say when you started doing it.
Explain when it usually happens in your day.
Describe what the routine looks like in practice.
Explain one main benefit with a small example.
Say why the habit is worth keeping.
Keep the lesson practical, not dramatic.

delivery_markers: What I like about this habit is ...
That is why I have managed to keep it.

samples.answer:
One habit that has become important in my daily life is going for a walk after dinner. I started doing it a few years ago because I used to stay inside all evening and feel tired after work.

Now I usually walk for about twenty or thirty minutes around my neighborhood. I do not treat it like serious exercise. It is just a simple way to move a little, get fresh air, and take a break from screens.

What makes this habit useful is that it helps me relax and clear my mind. On busy days, I often feel mentally full by the end of the evening. A short walk makes me feel calmer and helps me sleep better later. So it is not an exciting habit, but it is one that genuinely improves my routine.
highlights: Strong simple routine with clear benefit

opening: One habit that has become important is ...
body: I do not treat it like ...
closing: So it is not exciting, but ...

upgrades.improved: It helps me clear my mind after work.
upgrades.why: This gives the benefit more shape.
upgrades.note: Use after work, before bed, or in the morning for context.

upgrades.improved: I usually walk for about twenty or thirty minutes after dinner.
upgrades.why: Specific routine detail sounds more believable.
upgrades.note: Time markers make habits sound real.

samples.answer:
I would like to talk about a very simple habit I have built over the past year, which is taking a short walk after dinner. I began doing it at first for practical reasons, mainly because I was spending too much time sitting and scrolling on my phone in the evening.

These days I usually head outside for around twenty minutes, even if I am not especially energetic. What I like about it is that it is low-pressure and easy to maintain. I do not need special equipment or a lot of motivation, so it is a habit I can actually stick to.

A good example of its value is during stressful work periods. If I go straight from dinner back to my laptop, I feel tense for the rest of the night. But if I go for a walk first, it helps me wind down and set a calmer tone for the evening. That is why such a small habit has become surprisingly valuable to me.
highlights: Good explanation of why the habit is sustainable

opening: I would like to talk about a very simple habit ...
I began doing it for practical reasons ...
body: What I like about it is that ...
A good example of its value is ...
closing: That is why such a small habit has become ...

upgrades.improved: It is low-pressure and easy to maintain.
upgrades.why: This sounds more natural and more nuanced.
upgrades.note: Great for habits that last because they are manageable.

upgrades.improved: It helps me wind down and sets a calmer tone for the evening.
upgrades.why: The benefit is described through feeling and routine, not just result.

samples.answer:
A habit that quietly anchors my day is taking a short walk after dinner. It is not the sort of routine people usually describe as life-changing, but that is exactly why I value it: it is sustainable, low-effort, and consistently helpful.

I started doing it during a busy stretch at work when I noticed that my evenings had become shapeless. I would finish dinner, sit down with my phone or laptop, and somehow lose the rest of the night. The walk now acts as a small transition point. It breaks up the day, gives me a mental reset, and helps me return home feeling less mentally cluttered.

What matters most is that it works without requiring much discipline. I can still do it when I am tired, distracted, or short on motivation. In that sense, it is a small routine with a stabilizing effect, and I think that is why I have kept it for so long.
highlights: Band 8 answer uses subtle reflection and controlled phrasing
Anchors my day and stabilizing effect are strong but still teachable

opening: A habit that quietly anchors my day is ...
That is exactly why I value it ...
closing: In that sense, it is a small routine with ...

upgrades.original: It is part of my life.
upgrades.why: This is more vivid while staying concise.
upgrades.note: Use for habits that create structure.

upgrades.improved: It gives me a mental reset and a stabilizing effect.
upgrades.why: The impact becomes clearer and more mature.

practice.task: Describe one activity or habit that has become part of your life and explain why you keep it.
checklist: Say what the habit is and how it started.
Describe when and how you usually do it.
Explain one clear benefit with a small example.
self_prompts: How often do you do it?
What benefit do you notice most?
Why is it easy or hard to keep?
